threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 'the Good Egg' is just a cafe, not a pilgrimage. Locals know it's brunch royalty, not just another eatery.
Heard a couple arguing over who forgot the chips on the N35. She was *loudly* determined to make him regret it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a group of kids make a chalk mural on the sidewalk—an instant gallery that changed by the hour.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can find spontaneous poetry readings in the U-Bahn, complete with beats from nearby buskers—pure Berlin magic.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they flinch at the Civic Center BART closure but still have a backup route involving the 33 bus. It’s a whole strategy.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a ride with a busker belting out classic rock on the 5 today—his case full of coins almost outshining the Muni's delays.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can experience Gràcia just by visiting Parc Güell, but they miss the small plazas where locals share a vermut in the afternoons.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their rush to get off the metro at Diagonal, still startled by how it empties like a time machine.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You order a pint at the pub and ask for a ‘lager’, only to be met with a raised eyebrow. It’s all about the ales, mate.
Caught a glimpse of a lad in a full dinosaur costume on the N35 last night, doing a chicken dance. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted the old lady feeding pigeons at the playground while a group of kids practiced breakdancing nearby. That's Neukölln in a nutshell.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a kebab shop next to a gallery, where a local artist sells paintings while the scent of döner wafts through the air.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone's local when they can run from a barking dog on the tracks to catch the 14 without missing a beat. Pure instinct.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading a sea of Muni riders at Civic Center today; only in SF will you find a bus stop turned into a concert hall.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think ‘tapas’ is a meal, but locals know it’s just a snack before something hearty, like a proper ‘plat’ at noon.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their wide-eyed confusion at the metro map—it’s not a puzzle, just a labyrinth.
